title: ConfigurationTopTable Object
description: API object representing the top configuration table associated with a configured design. When obtained from the DataFile object of a configured design, the functionality is limited because it's not loaded in Fusion, and there is no access to the Fusion objects represented in the table. For example, any properties that return a Component or Parameter will return null because those objects aren't available.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getCell
methods.description: Returns the cell at the specified row and column.

methods.name: moveColumns
methods.description: Moves the specified columns from one table to another.

properties.name: activeRow
properties.description: Returns the row that is currently active. To set the active row, use the activate method on the ConfigurationRow object.

properties.name: appearanceTable
properties.description: Returns the appearance table associated with this top table. The returned table can be empty and not have any columns. In this case, the table is not displayed in the user interface. Use the returned table to add columns.

properties.name: columns
properties.description: Returns the columns defined for this table and provides the functionality to create new columns.

properties.name: customThemeTables
properties.description: Returns any custom theme tables associated with this top table.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique ID of this table.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: materialTable
properties.description: Returns the material table associated with this top table. The returned table can be empty and not have any columns. In this case, the table is not displayed in the user interface. Use the returned table to add columns.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets the name of the table as seen in the user interface.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: plasticRuleTable
properties.description: Returns the plastic rule table associated with this top table. The returned table can be empty and not have any columns. In this case, the table is not displayed in the user interface. Use the returned table to add columns.

properties.name: rows
properties.description: Returns the rows (configurations) defined for this table and provides the functionality to create new rows.

properties.name: sheetMetalRuleTable
properties.description: Returns the sheet metal rule table associated with this top table. The returned table can be empty and not have any columns. In this case, the table is not displayed in the user interface. Use the returned table to add columns.

classType.title: ConfigurationTopTable.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

getCell.title: ConfigurationTopTable.getCell Method
getCell.description: Returns the cell at the specified row and column.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: The index of the column the cell is in. An index of 0 is the first column and does not include the name column.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The index of the row the cell is in. An index of 0 is the first row and does not include the header row.

moveColumns.title: ConfigurationTopTable.moveColumns Method
moveColumns.description: Moves the specified columns from one table to another.
moveColumns.return_description: Returns an array of the columns created due to the move.

parameters.name: columns
parameters.description: An array of the columns within the top table you want to move.

parameters.name: targetTable
parameters.description: The table you want to move the columns to. The target must be a custom theme table.
